mission.title: contour: Please add support for wildcard virtual hosts
mission.description: Please add support for wildcard virtual hosts. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current contour deployment
steps.description:
Verify your contour version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
helm list -n contour 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working contour installation.

steps.title: Review contour configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant contour configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get configmap -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=contour
```
Hi,

I need multiple virtual host fqdn routed to the same backend service,

steps.title: Apply the fix for Please add support for wildcard virtual hosts
steps.description:
Similar to Ingress, Gateway APIs specify hostname matching here: https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/spec/#networking.x-k8s.io/v1alpha1.Listener

```
Hostname specifies the virtual hostname to match for protocol types that define this concept. When unspecified, “”, or *, all hostnames are matched. This field can be omitted for protocols that don’t require hostname based matching.

Hostname is the fully qualified domain name of a network host, as defined by RFC 3986. Note the following deviations

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get events -n contour --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Please add support for wildcard virtual hosts" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Similar to Ingress, Gateway APIs specify hostname matching here: https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/spec/#networking.x-k8s.io/v1alpha1.Listener

```
Hostname specifies the virtual hostname to match for protocol types that define this concept. When unspecified, “”, or *, all hostnames are matched. This field can be omitted for protocols that don’t require hostname based matching.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with contour installed or the issue environment reproducible.
